questions.q: Pain on inspiration when the examiner's fingers are hooked under the right costal margin. What is this sign?
questions.a: Murphy's Sign ➔ Indicates: Acute Cholecystitis (التهاب المرارة الحاد)

questions.q: Right lower quadrant pain elicited by deep palpation of the left lower quadrant. What is this sign?
questions.a: Rovsing's Sign ➔ Indicates: Acute Appendicitis (التهاب الزائدة الدودية)

questions.q: Superficial edema and bruising in the subcutaneous fatty tissue around the umbilicus. What is this sign?
questions.a: Cullen's Sign ➔ Indicates: Hemorrhagic Pancreatitis / Ectopic Pregnancy

questions.q: Bruising and discoloration of the flanks. What is this sign?
questions.a: Grey Turner's Sign ➔ Indicates: Retroperitoneal Hemorrhage / Hemorrhagic Pancreatitis

questions.q: A patient clenching their fist over their chest to describe ischemic chest pain. What is this sign?
questions.a: Levine's Sign ➔ Indicates: Myocardial Infarction / Angina

questions.q: Carpal spasm induced by inflating a blood pressure cuff above systolic pressure for 3 minutes. What is this sign?
questions.a: Trousseau's Sign of Tetany ➔ Indicates: Hypocalcemia

questions.q: Facial muscle twitching elicited by tapping the facial nerve anterior to the ear. What is this sign?
questions.a: Chvostek's Sign ➔ Indicates: Hypocalcemia

questions.q: Extension of the big toe and fanning of the other toes upon stroking the lateral plantar surface. What is this sign?
questions.a: Babinski's Sign ➔ Indicates: Upper Motor Neuron (UMN) Lesion

questions.q: Involuntary flexion of the hips and knees when the patient's neck is passively flexed. What is this sign?
questions.a: Brudzinski's Sign ➔ Indicates: Meningitis / Meningeal Irritation

questions.q: Resistance and pain upon extending the knee when the hip is flexed at 90 degrees. What is this sign?
questions.a: Kernig's Sign ➔ Indicates: Meningitis / Meningeal Irritation

questions.q: Calf pain upon forced dorsiflexion of the foot. What is this sign?
questions.a: Homan's Sign ➔ Indicates: Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT)

questions.q: Tingling sensation radiating into the fingers when the median nerve is tapped at the wrist. What is this sign?
questions.a: Tinel's Sign ➔ Indicates: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome

questions.q: Numbness or tingling in the median nerve distribution when the wrists are maximally flexed and held together for 60 seconds. What is this sign?
questions.a: Phalen's Sign ➔ Indicates: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome

questions.q: Rebound tenderness in the abdomen, indicating peritoneal inflammation. What is this sign?
questions.a: Blumberg's Sign ➔ Indicates: Peritonitis

questions.q: Referred pain to the left shoulder due to diaphragmatic irritation, especially when supine. What is this sign?
questions.a: Kehr's Sign ➔ Indicates: Splenic Rupture

questions.q: Painless, enlarged, palpable gallbladder with jaundice. What is this sign?
questions.a: Courvoisier's Sign ➔ Indicates: Pancreatic Head Cancer / Cholangiocarcinoma

questions.q: Bluish discoloration of the cervix, vagina, and labia during early pregnancy. What is this sign?
questions.a: Chadwick's Sign ➔ Indicates: Early Pregnancy

questions.q: Softening of the lower uterine segment during early pregnancy. What is this sign?
questions.a: Hegar's Sign ➔ Indicates: Early Pregnancy

questions.q: Sloughing of the epidermis upon applying slight lateral pressure to the skin. What is this sign?
questions.a: Nikolsky's Sign ➔ Indicates: Pemphigus Vulgaris / TEN / SSSS

questions.q: Pinpoint bleeding after the removal of a scale from a skin plaque. What is this sign?
questions.a: Auspitz Sign ➔ Indicates: Psoriasis

questions.q: Tiny white spots on a red background on the buccal mucosa. What are these spots called?
questions.a: Koplik's Spots ➔ Indicates: Measles (الحصبة)

questions.q: Loss of balance when standing with eyes closed and feet together. What is this sign?
questions.a: Romberg's Sign ➔ Indicates: Sensory Ataxia / Posterior Column Lesion

questions.q: Dropping of the contralateral pelvis when standing on one leg. What is this sign?
questions.a: Trendelenburg's Sign ➔ Indicates: Gluteus Medius Weakness / Superior Gluteal Nerve Palsy

questions.q: A patient uses their hands to 'walk up' their own legs to stand up from a squatted position. What is this sign?
questions.a: Gowers' Sign ➔ Indicates: Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy

questions.q: Right lower quadrant pain upon extension of the right hip. What is this sign?
questions.a: Psoas Sign ➔ Indicates: Retrocecal Appendicitis

questions.q: Right lower quadrant pain upon internal rotation of the flexed right hip. What is this sign?
questions.a: Obturator Sign ➔ Indicates: Pelvic Appendicitis

questions.q: Pain referred to the epigastrium upon continuous firm pressure over McBurney's point. What is this sign?
questions.a: Aaron's Sign ➔ Indicates: Acute Appendicitis

questions.q: Hyperesthesia (increased sensitivity) below the right scapula. What is this sign?
questions.a: Boas' Sign ➔ Indicates: Acute Cholecystitis

questions.q: Flexion of the thumb and index finger when the nail of the middle finger is flicked downward. What is this sign?
questions.a: Hoffmann's Sign ➔ Indicates: Cervical UMN Lesion

questions.q: Shifting dullness and fluid wave on abdominal percussion. What does this indicate?
questions.a: Puddle Sign / Fluid Wave ➔ Indicates: Ascites

questions.q: A palpable, enlarged, hard lymph node in the left supraclavicular fossa. What is this node called?
questions.a: Virchow's Node (Troisier's Sign) ➔ Indicates: Gastric or Intra-abdominal Malignancy

questions.q: A palpable firm nodule at the umbilicus. What is this sign?
questions.a: Sister Mary Joseph Nodule ➔ Indicates: Intra-abdominal / Pelvic Malignancy

questions.q: Sustained blinking when tapping the glabella repeatedly. What is this sign?
questions.a: Myerson's Sign ➔ Indicates: Parkinson's Disease

questions.q: A diagonal crease across the earlobe. What is this sign?
questions.a: Frank's Sign ➔ Indicates: Coronary Artery Disease (CAD)

questions.q: Painful, erythematous, raised nodules on the pads of the fingers and toes. What is this sign?
questions.a: Osler's Nodes ➔ Indicates: Infective Endocarditis

questions.q: Painless, erythematous macules on the palms and soles. What is this sign?
questions.a: Janeway Lesions ➔ Indicates: Infective Endocarditis

questions.q: Retinal hemorrhages with pale or white centers seen on fundoscopy. What are these called?
questions.a: Roth Spots ➔ Indicates: Infective Endocarditis / Leukemia

questions.q: Capillary pulsations visible in the nail beds upon applying slight pressure. What is this sign?
questions.a: Quincke's Sign ➔ Indicates: Severe Aortic Regurgitation

questions.q: A rapidly rising and forcefully collapsing arterial pulse ('water-hammer' pulse). What is this sign?
questions.a: Corrigan's Pulse ➔ Indicates: Aortic Regurgitation

questions.q: Rhythmic nodding of the head in time with the heartbeat. What is this sign?
questions.a: De Musset's Sign ➔ Indicates: Aortic Regurgitation

questions.q: Systolic pulsations of the uvula. What is this sign?
questions.a: Müller's Sign ➔ Indicates: Aortic Regurgitation

questions.q: Pistol-shot sounds heard over the femoral arteries on auscultation. What is this sign?
questions.a: Traube's Sign ➔ Indicates: Aortic Regurgitation

questions.q: A systolic and diastolic murmur heard over the femoral artery when compressed by a stethoscope. What is this sign?
questions.a: Duroziez's Sign ➔ Indicates: Aortic Regurgitation

questions.q: Popliteal systolic blood pressure exceeding brachial systolic pressure by more than 20-60 mmHg. What is this sign?
questions.a: Hill's Sign ➔ Indicates: Severe Aortic Regurgitation

questions.q: Visible pulsations of the retinal arteries and pupils. What is this sign?
questions.a: Becker's Sign ➔ Indicates: Aortic Regurgitation

questions.q: A paradoxical increase in jugular venous pressure (JVP) during inspiration. What is this sign?
questions.a: Kussmaul's Sign ➔ Indicates: Constrictive Pericarditis / Right Ventricular Failure

questions.q: Dullness to percussion, bronchial breathing, and egophony at the angle of the left scapula. What is this sign?
questions.a: Ewart's Sign ➔ Indicates: Large Pericardial Effusion

questions.q: Paradoxical inward movement of the lower rib cage during inspiration. What is this sign?
questions.a: Hoover's Sign (Pulmonary) ➔ Indicates: Severe COPD

questions.q: Weakness of leg extension that paradoxically improves when tested against resistance in the opposite leg. What is this sign?
questions.a: Hoover's Sign (Neurology) ➔ Indicates: Functional / Psychogenic Weakness (Malingering)

questions.q: A crunching sound heard over the precordium, synchronized with the heartbeat. What is this sign?
questions.a: Hamman's Sign (Hamman's Crunch) ➔ Indicates: Pneumomediastinum

questions.q: Lid lag on downward gaze. What is this sign?
questions.a: Von Graefe's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease (Thyrotoxicosis)

questions.q: Infrequent or incomplete blinking of the eyes. What is this sign?
questions.a: Stellwag's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Inability to maintain convergence of the eyes. What is this sign?
questions.a: Möbius' Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Absence of forehead wrinkling when the patient looks upwards. What is this sign?
questions.a: Joffroy's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Pigmented iris hamartomas seen on slit-lamp examination. What are these called?
questions.a: Lisch Nodules ➔ Indicates: Neurofibromatosis Type 1

questions.q: Copper deposition forming a dark ring around the periphery of the cornea. What is this sign?
questions.a: Kayser-Fleischer Ring ➔ Indicates: Wilson's Disease

questions.q: Small white or grayish-brown spots on the periphery of the iris in infants. What are these called?
questions.a: Brushfield Spots ➔ Indicates: Down Syndrome

questions.q: Pupils that accommodate to near vision but do not react to light. What is this sign?
questions.a: Argyll Robertson Pupil (Prostitute's Pupil) ➔ Indicates: Neurosyphilis

questions.q: A pupil that paradoxically dilates when a flashlight is swung from the normal eye to the affected eye. What is this sign?
questions.a: Marcus Gunn Pupil (RAPD) ➔ Indicates: Optic Neuritis / Severe Retinal Disease

questions.q: A unilateral dilated pupil with sluggish light reflex and slow accommodation, usually with absent ankle jerks. What is this sign?
questions.a: Holmes-Adie Pupil ➔ Indicates: Adie Syndrome

questions.q: A 'cracked-pot' sound heard on percussion of the skull in children. What is this sign?
questions.a: Macewen's Sign ➔ Indicates: Hydrocephalus

questions.q: Migratory superficial thrombophlebitis. What is this sign?
questions.a: Trousseau's Syndrome of Malignancy ➔ Indicates: Pancreatic / Gastric Adenocarcinoma

questions.q: Relief of scrotal pain upon elevating the affected testis. What is this sign?
questions.a: Prehn's Sign ➔ Indicates: Epididymitis (Negative in Testicular Torsion)

questions.q: Bruising over the inguinal ligament. What is this sign?
questions.a: Fox's Sign ➔ Indicates: Hemorrhagic Pancreatitis

questions.q: Sudden appearance of multiple seborrheic keratoses on the skin. What is this sign?
questions.a: Leser-Trélat Sign ➔ Indicates: Underlying Gastrointestinal Adenocarcinoma

questions.q: Pain elicited by placing the thumb inside the flexed fingers and ulnar deviating the wrist. What is this test?
questions.a: Finkelstein's Test ➔ Indicates: De Quervain's Tenosynovitis

questions.q: Flexion of the thumb IP joint when attempting to hold a piece of paper between the thumb and index finger against resistance. What is this sign?
questions.a: Froment's Sign ➔ Indicates: Ulnar Nerve Palsy

questions.q: Fusiform swelling, flexed posture, tenderness along the tendon sheath, and severe pain on passive extension. What are these signs collectively called?
questions.a: Kanavel's Signs ➔ Indicates: Suppurative Flexor Tenosynovitis

questions.q: A clicking or locking sensation during rotation of the flexed knee under varus/valgus stress. What is this test?
questions.a: McMurray's Test ➔ Indicates: Meniscal Tear

questions.q: Excessive anterior translation of the tibia under the femur with the knee flexed at 20-30 degrees. What is this test?
questions.a: Lachman's Test ➔ Indicates: Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) Tear

questions.q: Reduced expansion of the lumbar spine during forward flexion (increases less than 5 cm). What is this test?
questions.a: Schober's Test ➔ Indicates: Ankylosing Spondylitis

questions.q: Transient worsening of neurological symptoms with an increase in body temperature (e.g., after a hot shower). What is this phenomenon?
questions.a: Uhthoff's Phenomenon ➔ Indicates: Multiple Sclerosis

questions.q: Sudden onset of vertigo and nystagmus elicited by rapidly moving from a sitting to a supine position with the head turned 45 degrees. What is this test?
questions.a: Dix-Hallpike Maneuver ➔ Indicates: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (BPPV)

questions.q: Calluses or scars on the knuckles or dorsum of the hand from repeated self-induced vomiting. What is this sign?
questions.a: Russell's Sign ➔ Indicates: Bulimia Nervosa

questions.q: Abdominal pain that remains unchanged or increases when the patient tenses their abdominal muscles. What is this sign?
questions.a: Carnett's Sign ➔ Indicates: Abdominal Wall Pathology (e.g., Rectus Sheath Hematoma)

questions.q: A palpable abdominal mass that does not cross the midline and remains palpable when the rectus muscles are tensed. What is this sign?
questions.a: Fothergill's Sign ➔ Indicates: Rectus Sheath Hematoma

questions.q: Pain along the inner aspect of the thigh extending to the knee, exacerbated by internal rotation of the hip. What is this sign?
questions.a: Howship-Romberg Sign ➔ Indicates: Obturator Hernia

questions.q: Absence of bowel sounds or a feeling of emptiness in the right lower quadrant upon palpation. What is this sign?
questions.a: Dance's Sign ➔ Indicates: Intussusception

questions.q: A venous hum heard over the umbilicus due to collateral circulation. What is this sign?
questions.a: Cruveilhier-Baumgarten Sign ➔ Indicates: Portal Hypertension

questions.q: A palpable, firm, shelf-like tumor felt on the anterior rectal wall during a digital rectal examination. What is this sign?
questions.a: Blumer's Shelf ➔ Indicates: Metastatic Pelvic Cancer (Pouch of Douglas metastasis)

questions.q: Downward tug of the trachea synchronous with cardiac systole felt when lifting the cricoid cartilage. What is this sign?
questions.a: Oliver's Sign (Tracheal Tug) ➔ Indicates: Aortic Arch Aneurysm

questions.q: Lateral pulsation of the trachea felt during physical examination. What is this sign?
questions.a: Cardarelli's Sign ➔ Indicates: Aortic Arch Aneurysm

questions.q: An exaggerated fall in systolic blood pressure (>10 mmHg) during normal inspiration. What is this sign?
questions.a: Pulsus Paradoxus ➔ Indicates: Cardiac Tamponade / Severe Asthma / Constrictive Pericarditis

questions.q: A rapid fall and rise of the JVP waveform (prominent, steep 'y' descent). What is this sign?
questions.a: Friedreich's Sign ➔ Indicates: Constrictive Pericarditis

questions.q: Abnormal widening of the palpebral fissure causing a staring appearance. What is this sign?
questions.a: Dalrymple's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Delayed descent of the lower eyelid on downward gaze. What is this sign?
questions.a: Griffith's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Spasmodic, jerky lowering of the upper eyelids on downward gaze. What is this sign?
questions.a: Boston's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Edema and puffiness of the eyelids, especially the upper eyelid. What is this sign?
questions.a: Enroth's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Difficulty everting the upper eyelid. What is this sign?
questions.a: Gifford's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Fine tremor of the gently closed eyelids. What is this sign?
questions.a: Rosenbach's Sign ➔ Indicates: Graves' Disease

questions.q: Urticaria and blistering appearing upon stroking a maculopapular skin lesion. What is this sign?
questions.a: Darier's Sign ➔ Indicates: Mastocytosis / Urticaria Pigmentosa

questions.q: Axillary or inguinal freckling. What is this sign?
questions.a: Crowe's Sign ➔ Indicates: Neurofibromatosis Type 1

questions.q: A vesicular rash on the tip of the nose, indicating involvement of the nasociliary branch of the ophthalmic nerve. What is this sign?
questions.a: Hutchinson's Sign (Nose) ➔ Indicates: Herpes Zoster Ophthalmicus

questions.q: Peg-shaped, notched, and widely spaced permanent incisors. What is this sign?
questions.a: Hutchinson's Teeth ➔ Indicates: Congenital Syphilis

questions.q: A painful click felt upon squeezing the metatarsal heads together. What is this sign?
questions.a: Mulder's Sign ➔ Indicates: Morton's Neuroma

questions.q: Abduction of the little finger caused by unopposed action of the extensor digiti minimi. What is this sign?
questions.a: Wartenberg's Sign ➔ Indicates: Ulnar Nerve Neuropathy

questions.q: Abdominal pain elicited by dropping from standing on the toes to the heels with a jarring landing. What is this sign?
questions.a: Markle Sign (Heel Drop) ➔ Indicates: Acute Appendicitis / Peritonitis

questions.q: Extreme lower abdominal and pelvic pain causing the patient to reach up or jump when the cervix is manipulated. What is this sign?
questions.a: Chandelier Sign (Cervical Motion Tenderness) ➔ Indicates: Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (PID)

questions.q: Simultaneous fever and relative bradycardia. What is this sign called?
questions.a: Faget's Sign ➔ Indicates: Yellow Fever / Typhoid Fever / Tularemia
